title: I release money shame. I release it now.
subtitle: A three-minute release-and-allow for guilt and regret around past financial decisions.
excerpt: A three-minute pair of energy statements for the money shame loop: the replaying of past decisions, the overspending or missed investment or period of financial chaos, and the guilt that keeps those moments active today. Use when old shame is loud, not when making a current decision.
sourceNotes: Release / Allow structure: The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024), chapters 2 through 5. Theme adapted from Day 5 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Release shame about past mistakes." The combination as a daytime reset is original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use when a past money decision is replaying and the shame from it is active today — not when reviewing current finances or making a decision.

content.text: A three-minute pair of energy statements for the money shame loop: the replaying of old decisions, a period of overspending, a missed opportunity, a time when the numbers went wrong, and the guilt that keeps those moments active today. Use it when the old shame is loud, not when reviewing current finances.

attrs.quote: I am ready to release the shame and regret I carry around past money decisions. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow forgiveness, growth, and the knowing that I know more now and choose differently. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

content.text: Say each three times, slow, out loud or in your mind.
If the shame is heavy and the allow statement won't move, tap first.
works directly on the regret loop before the reframe. For a shorter affirmation to carry through the day,
I did the best I could with what I knew
is a one-line repeat to return to when the voice starts again.

The release-and-allow structure is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024), chapters 2 through 5. The theme is adapted from Day 5 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The combination as a daytime reset is original to the homemade.education library.
